office_does: One of 49 state senators; writes and votes on state law, adopts state budgets, and sets statewide policy for a four-year term.
race_blurb: Both candidates advance to the general regardless of the primary result. The Democratic incumbent, a committee chair with a legislative record dating to 2017, faces a first-time Republican candidate whose file is pamphlet-only.

candidates.summary: Incumbent senator and chair of the Senate State Government, Tribal Affairs & Elections Committee, with legislative service since 2017 and a signature record on gun regulation.
highlights: Wrote laws banning ghost guns and high-capacity magazines; sponsored SB 5098 restricting guns in parks and fairgrounds [S1][S4]
Voted to tax millionaires and the very wealthy to fund school lunches, small-business relief, and healthcare [S1]
Says he is leading efforts to ban ICE masking, bar surveillance of immigrants, and block federal access to state voter data [S1]

experience.basis: Legislator since 2017 (House then Senate) with a committee chairmanship and enacted record.

taxes.basis: Voted to tax millionaires and the very wealthy to fund school lunches, small-business relief, and healthcare investments.

spending.basis: Frames new wealth revenue as funding expanded services (school lunches, healthcare investments).

housing.basis: Supported a cap on rent increases alongside zoning reform for more affordable housing — mixed means with a tenant-protection component.

immigration.basis: Leading efforts to ban ICE agents from masking, bar camera surveillance of immigrants, and block federal access to state voter data.

social.basis: Helped backfill state funding for Planned Parenthood after federal cuts; endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates.

climate.basis: Endorsed by Washington Conservation Action; no direct climate-policy statements in the dossier.

candidates.summary: Retired registered nurse making her first run for public office; her statement centers on claims of fiscal mismanagement in state government, and her evidence file is pamphlet-only.
highlights: Retired RN with a career spanning hospitals, mental health home care, and 16 years as charge nurse at UW Northwest Hospital [S1]
States a belief in 'the sacredness of life'; twice elected to the National Association of Pro-Life Nurses (1988-1992) [S1]
Attributes rising taxes and declining services to what she characterizes as theft of taxpayer funds through unaudited nonprofits [S1]

experience.basis: First-time legislative candidate with no public-office record; background is nursing and community volunteering.

taxes.basis: Frames rising taxes as a harm produced by corruption and misdirected funds.

spending.basis: Centers her statement on unaudited nonprofit funding channels and alleged theft of taxpayer funds — an audit-and-accountability platform.

social.basis: States a belief in 'the sacredness of life' and served two terms as an officer of the National Association of Pro-Life Nurses.
